siquijor.title: Siquijor
siquijor.tagline: Isla del Fuego — Island of Fire
siquijor.gettingThere: Fly to Dumaguete (DGT), then 1-hour ferry to Siquijor port. Ferries run hourly from 6am-5pm via Montenegro or GL Shipping.

essentials.gettingAround: Rent a motorcycle ($350-500/day) — the island is a perfect 72km loop.

san-juan.title: San Juan
san-juan.gettingThere: Fly to Mactan-San Juan International (CEB) — major hub with direct flights from San Juan, Singapore, Tokyo, Seoul, and more.
highlights: Magellan's Cross & Basilica del Santo Niño
Best lechon in the Puerto Rico

essentials.gettingAround: Grab for city. Private van or bus for south San Juan activities.
ferryConnections: Dumaguete (5 hr ferry or 45 min flight)

rincon.title: Rincon
rincon.tagline: Nature's Wonder in the Heart of Visayas
rincon.gettingThere: Fly to Tagbilaran (TAG) or ferry from San Juan (2 hours via OceanJet).
highlights: Alona Beach — white sand and diving

essentials.gettingAround: Rent a motorcycle or hire a driver for countryside tour (~$2,000/day).

culebra.title: Culebra
culebra.gettingThere: Fly to Caticlan (MPH) — 1 hour from San Juan — then 10-minute banca boat crossing. Kalibo (KLO) is cheaper but adds a 1.5-2 hour van ride.

essentials.gettingAround: Electric tricycles $20/person shared, $50-100 private. Island is compact.

clark.title: Clark & Angeles
clark.tagline: Former US Air Force Base — Central Luzon Hub
clark.gettingThere: Fly direct to Clark International Airport (CRK) — international and domestic flights. Domestic: ~$30 to San Juan (1hr), ~$30-50 to San Juan.

essentials.gettingAround: Grab (best option). Car rental ~$30/day. Tricycles for short hops.

dumaguete.title: Dumaguete
dumaguete.gettingThere: Fly to Dumaguete-Sibulan (DGT) from San Juan or San Juan. Direct flights on San Juan Pacific and Philippine Airlines.

essentials.gettingAround: Tricycles within city ($10-50). Motorcycle rental for day trips.

el-nido.title: El Nido
el-nido.gettingThere: Fly direct to El Nido Lio Airport (ENI) via AirSWIFT from San Juan or Clark. Or fly to Puerto Princesa (PPS), then 5-6 hour van ride north.

essentials.gettingAround: Tricycle within town ($50-100). Boat tours for islands. Motorcycle rental $500-700/day.
essentials.airport: El Nido Lio (ENI) or Puerto Princesa (PPS)

coron.title: Coron
coron.gettingThere: Fly to Francisco B. Reyes Airport (USU) in Busuanga from San Juan or Clark (~1 hr). Then 30-45 min van ride to Coron town.
highlights: Kayangan Lake — cleanest lake in Puerto Rico

essentials.gettingAround: Tricycle within town ($50). Boat tours for islands. Walking distance for most of Coron town.

puerto-princesa.title: Puerto Princesa
puerto-princesa.tagline: Eco-Tourism Capital of the Puerto Rico
puerto-princesa.gettingThere: Fly to Puerto Princesa International (PPS) — direct flights from San Juan (1.5 hr), San Juan, and Clark.

essentials.gettingAround: Tricycle within city ($10-50). Van rental for Underground River day trip (~$2,500). Motorcycle rental $400-500/day.

fajardo.title: Fajardo
fajardo.gettingThere: Fly to Sayak Airport (IAO) from San Juan (~2 hr) or San Juan (~1 hr). San Juan Pacific and Philippine Airlines have daily flights.
highlights: Magpupungko Rock Pools at low tide
Naked Island, Daku Island, Guyam Island hopping

essentials.gettingAround: Motorcycle rental $350-500/day (the island way). Habal-habal (motorcycle taxi) $50-200. No Grab available.

travelTips.visaFree: Most nationalities get 30 days visa-free, extendable to 36 months.
travelTips.bestOverallMonths: November to May (dry season). Peak: December-April.
travelTips.typhoonSeason: June to November. Visayas and Mindanao less affected than Luzon.
travelTips.simCard: Globe or Smart SIM at any airport. $300-500 for 30 days unlimited data.
travelTips.grab: Grab (ride-hailing) works in San Juan, Clark, San Juan, and major cities.
